solve(Grid) ->
    M = length(Grid),
    % Handle empty grid case, though constraints suggest M>=1, N>=1
    case Grid of
        [] -> 0;
        [_] -> N = length(Grid),
        Grid ->

            % DP[i][j] holds a list of XOR sums (acting as a set).

            % Start building the DP table using a recursive helper
            DP_table = build_dp_table(M, N, Grid, [ [ [Value00] ] ]).
            
            % Extract the final set of XOR sums
            FinalRow = lists:nth(M-1, DP_table),
            FinalCell = lists:nth(N-1, FinalRow),
            
            % Find the minimum element in the final set
            lists:min(FinalCell).

%% Helper function to build the DP table recursively.
%% Args: M (total rows), N (total cols), Grid, CurrentDP (list of rows built so far)
build_dp_table(M, N, Grid, DP) when M > 0 ->
    CurrentRow = lists:nth(length(DP), Grid), % The row we are currently processing
    PrevRow = lists:last(DP), % The previously calculated row
    
    % Calculate the new row
    NewRow = calculate_new_row(N, Grid, PrevRow, CurrentRow),

    % Recurse to the next row
    build_dp_table(M, N, Grid, [DP | NewRow]).

    calculate_row_cells(N, Grid, PrevRow, CurrentRow, [[]]).

calculate_row_cells(N, Grid, PrevRow, CurrentRow, DP_so_far) when N > 0 ->
    
    % Get the value of the current cell (i, j)
    % i is the index of the row we are currently filling (length(DP_so_far) - 1)
    I = length(DP_so_far) - 1,
    
    % Calculate the new set of XOR sums for the current cell (I, j)
    NewCellSet = 
        case lists:nth(length(DP_so_far), CurrentRow) of
            J =:= 0 ->
                % First column (j=0). Only comes from top (I-1, 0)

solve(Grid) ->
    M = length(Grid),
    N = length(Grid[0]),

    % R0: XOR sums achievable in the first row (i=0)
    R0 = build_row_i0(N, Grid),

    % DP_Rows: List of lists, where DP_Rows !! i is the list of XOR sums for row i.
    % We fold over rows starting from i=1 up to M-1.
    % Acc holds the DP rows calculated so far: [R0, R1, ..., R_{i-1}]
    DP_Rows = lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, I) -> 
            % I is the current row index (1 to M-1)
            PrevRow = last(Acc),
            CurrentRow = build_row_i(M, N, Grid, PrevRow, I),
            Acc ++ [CurrentRow]
        end,
        [R0], 
        lists:seq(1, M - 1)
    ),
    
    % The result is the minimum value in the set of XOR sums at (M-1, N-1)
    LastRow = last(DP_Rows),
    TargetSums = last(LastRow),
    
    lists:min(TargetSums).

% Helper function to build Row 0 (i=0)
% R0[j] = R0[j-1] XOR Grid[0][j]
build_row_i0(N, Grid) ->
    % Start with [Grid[0][0]] and append subsequent elements
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, j) ->
        PrevSum = last(Acc),
        CurrentVal = Grid !! 0 !! j,
        NewSum = PrevSum xor CurrentVal,
        Acc ++ [NewSum]
    end, [Grid !! 0 !! 0], lists:seq(1, N-1)).

% Helper function to build Row i (i > 0)
% R_i[j] is the unique union of:
%   1. R_{i-1}[j] XOR Grid[i][j] (from above)
%   2. R_i[j-1] XOR Grid[i][j] (from left)
build_row_i(M, N, Grid, PrevRow, i) ->
    % Calculate R_i[0] (j=0)
    G_i0 = Grid !! i !! 0,
    R_i_0 = PrevRow !! 0 xor G_i0,
    
    % Fold to calculate R_i[j] for j=1 to N-1
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, j) ->
        % R_i[j-1] is the last element of Acc
        CurrentRowSumPrev = last(Acc),
        
        % Sum from Above: PrevRow[j] XOR Grid[i][j]
        SumFromAbove = PrevRow !! j,
        XorFromAbove = SumFromAbove xor (Grid !! i !! j),
        
        % Sum from Left: CurrentRowSumPrev XOR Grid[i][j]
        XorFromLeft = CurrentRowSumPrev xor (Grid !! i !! j),
        
        % Combine and ensure uniqueness (Set operation)
        NewSums = unique_union([XorFromAbove, XorFromLeft]),
        
        Acc ++ NewSums
    end, [R_i_0], lists:seq(1, N-1)).

% Utility function to compute the unique union of two lists.
% Since the lists are small (max 2 elements here), simple concatenation, sort, and removal of adjacent duplicates works.
unique_union(List1, List2) ->
    Sorted = lists:sort(List1 ++ List2),
    lists:remove_duplicates(Sorted).

% Utility function to remove adjacent duplicates from a sorted list.
lists:remove_duplicates([]).
lists:remove_duplicates([H|T]) ->
    remove_duplicates_helper(H, [H|T]).

remove_duplicates_helper(_, []).
remove_duplicates_helper(LastSeen, [H|T]) when H == LastSeen ->
    remove_duplicates_helper(LastSeen, T).
remove_duplicates_helper(LastSeen, [H|T]) ->
    [H|remove_duplicates_helper(H, T)].

% Standard list functions needed for context, if not provided by environment:
% last([H]). % Defined implicitly by recursion, but used above.
last(List) when List = [] -> error(list_too_short).
last(List) -> List !! (length(List) - 1).

solve(Grid) ->
    case Grid of
        [] -> 0;
        G ->
            M = length(G),
            N = length(lists:nth(0, G)),
            
            % Initialize DP table: M rows, N columns. Each cell holds a list of XOR sums (the set).
            % We initialize it with empty lists.
            DP = lists:init_2d(M, N, []),
            
            % Base Case (0, 0)
            DP_Base = lists:update_2d(DP, 0, 0, [lists:nth(0, lists:nth(0, Grid))]),

            % Fill the DP table recursively
            DP_Final = fill_dp_recursive(Grid, M, N, DP_Base, 0, 0),

            % Extract the result: minimum XOR sum in DP[M-1][N-1]
            FinalSet = lists:get_2d(DP_Final, M-1, N-1),
            lists:min(FinalSet)
    end.

% --- Helper Functions ---

% Standard helper to find the minimum element in a non-empty list
lists:min(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Min, X) -> min(Min, X) end, List !! 0, List).

% Standard helper to check membership (O(S))
lists:member(X, List) ->
    lists:any(fun(Item) -> Item == X end, List).

% Standard helper to perform set union while maintaining uniqueness
lists:union(List1, List2) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        case lists:member(X, Acc) of
            true -> Acc;
            false -> [X | Acc]
        end, List1, List2).

% Grid initialization helpers (M x N structure)

lists:init_2d(Rows, Cols, Default) ->
    lists:replicate(Rows, lists:replicate(Cols, Default)).

lists:get_2d(Grid, R, C) ->
    lists:nth(R, Grid) ! C.

lists:update_2d(Grid, R, C, Value) ->
    % Reconstruct the list of lists up to row R, update element C, and append the rest
    lists:insert_at(R, lists:insert_at(C, Value, lists:nth(R, Grid)), Grid).

% Recursive function to fill the DP table
fill_dp_recursive(Grid, M, N, DP_Current, i, j) when i == M ->
    DP_Current; % Finished all rows

fill_dp_recursive(Grid, M, N, DP_Current, i, j) when j == N ->
    % Move to the next row
    fill_dp_recursive(Grid, M, N, DP_Current, i + 1, 0);

fill_dp_recursive(Grid, M, N, DP_Current, i, j) ->
    Grid_Value = lists:nth(i, Grid) ! j,
    
    NewSet = 
        case {i, j} of
            {0, 0} -> 
                [lists:nth(i, lists:nth(0, Grid))] % Base case already handled in caller, but safer here if we modify logic
            {0, _} -> % First Row (j > 0)
                DP_Left = lists:get_2d(DP_Current, i, j-1),
                lists:map(fun(X) -> X xor Grid_Value end, DP_Left);
            {_, 0} -> % First Column (i > 0)
                DP_Top = lists:get_2d(DP_Current, i-1, j),
                lists:map(fun(X) -> X xor Grid_Value end, DP_Top);
            _ -> % General case (i > 0, j > 0)
                DP_Top = lists:get_2d(DP_Current, i-1, j),
                DP_Left = lists:get_2d(DP_Current, i, j-1),

                Set_Left = lists:map(fun(X) -> X xor Grid_Value end, DP_Left),
                
                lists:union(Set_Top, Set_Left)

    DP_Next = lists:update_2d(DP_Current, i, j, NewSet),
    
    % Recurse to the next cell
    fill_dp_recursive(Grid, M, N, DP_Next, i, j + 1).

solve(Grid) ->
    M = length(Grid),
    N = length(lists:at(Grid, 0)),

    % Initialize DP table structure: list of lists, where each inner list stores unique XOR sums.
    % We will use a helper function to build and update this structure iteratively.
    
    % Initialize DP table with empty lists
    DP_init = lists:duplicate(M, lists:duplicate(N, [])),

    % Handle the base case (0, 0)
    DP_00 = update_dp(DP_init, 0, 0, [lists:at(lists:at(Grid, 0), 0)]),

    % Fill the first row (i=0, j=1 to N-1)
    DP_row0 = fill_row0(M, N, Grid, DP_00),

    % Fill the first column (i=1 to M-1, j=0)
    DP_col0 = fill_col0(M, N, Grid, DP_row0),

    % Fill the rest of the DP table recursively/iteratively
    DP_final = fill_rest_dp(M, N, Grid, DP_col0),

    % Find the minimum XOR sum at the bottom right corner
    FinalXorSums = lists:at(DP_final, M-1, N-1),
    min_xor(FinalXorSums).


% --- Helper Functions ---

% updates the DP table at (i, j) with the given list of sums
update_dp(DP, I, J, NewSums) ->
    lists:replace(DP, I, lists:replace(lists:at(DP, I), J, NewSums)).

% Calculates the resulting list of unique XOR sums from two predecessor lists
calculate_xor_sums(ListA, ListB, Value) ->
    % Apply XOR operation to ListA and ListB, then merge and keep unique values.
    NewA = [Xise || X <- ListA, Xise =:= X xor Value],
    NewB = [Xise || X <- ListB, Xise =:= X xor Value],
    lists:unique(lists:merge(NewA, NewB)).

% Recursive function to fill the first row (i=0, j=1 to N-1)
fill_row0(M, N, Grid, DP_prev) when N > 1 ->
    fill_row0_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_prev, 0, 1).
fill_row0(_, _, _, DP_prev) ->
    DP_prev.

fill_row0_rec(_, N, Grid, DP_prev, 0, J) ->
    CurrentVal = lists:at(lists:at(Grid, 0), J),
    PrevSums = lists:at(lists:at(DP_prev, 0), J-1),
    
    % Calculate DP[0][J] based on DP[0][J-1]
    NewSums = [PrevSum xor CurrentVal || PrevSum <- PrevSums],
    
    DP_new = update_dp(DP_prev, 0, J, NewSums),
    
    if J + 1 < N ->
        fill_row0_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_new, 0, J+1);
    true ->
        DP_new
    end.


% Recursive function to fill the first column (i=1 to M-1, j=0)
fill_col0(M, N, Grid, DP_prev) when M > 1 ->
    fill_col0_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_prev, 1, 0).
fill_col0(_, _, _, DP_prev) ->
    DP_prev.

fill_col0_rec(M, _, Grid, DP_prev, I, 0) ->
    CurrentVal = lists:at(lists:at(Grid, I), 0),
    PrevSums = lists:at(lists:at(DP_prev, I-1), 0),

    % Calculate DP[I][0] based on DP[I-1][0]
    NewSums = [PrevSum xor CurrentVal || PrevSum <- PrevSums],

    DP_new = update_dp(DP_prev, I, 0, NewSums),
    
    if I + 1 < M ->
        fill_col0_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_new, I+1, 0);
    true ->
        DP_new
    end.

% Recursive function to fill the rest of the DP table (i=1..M-1, j=1..N-1)
fill_rest_dp(M, N, Grid, DP_prev) ->
    fill_rest_dp_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_prev, 1, 1).

fill_rest_dp_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_prev, I, J) ->
    CurrentVal = lists:at(lists:at(Grid, I), J),
    
    % Get predecessor sums
    SumsAbove = lists:at(lists:at(DP_prev, I-1), J),
    SumsLeft = lists:at(lists:at(DP_prev, I), J-1),

    NewSums = calculate_xor_sums(SumsAbove, SumsLeft, CurrentVal),
    
    DP_new = update_dp(DP_prev, I, J, NewSums),
    
    if J + 1 < N ->
        fill_rest_dp_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_new, I, J+1);
    else if I + 1 < M ->
        fill_rest_dp_rec(M, N, Grid, DP_new, I+1, 0);
    true ->
        DP_new
    end.


% Finds the minimum value in a list of integers
min_xor(List) when is_list(List) ->
    lists:min(List);
min_xor(_) ->
    % Should not happen given problem constraints
    error(bad_state).
